answer:
\boxed{B}

The data shows no statistical difference in survival between patients with high (400–899 IU/ml) and very high (≥900 IU/ml) CA 19-9 levels, nor does it indicate that very high levels are a contraindication. Survival outcomes were worse only when levels remained elevated preoperatively, not due to the absolute level itself. Thus, very high CA 19-9 alone does not disqualify patients from surgery.
